judgment.summary: Lululemon shows strong historical returns and margins but faces current headwinds with declining revenue and earnings growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter beat EPS estimates by 4.8%; next earnings date is 2026-06-04.
positives: Trailing P/E 9.56x and Forward P/E 9.58x suggest a potentially undervalued stock relative to earnings.
PEG ratio of 0.60 indicates potential undervaluation relative to expected earnings growth.
Gross margin 56.6%, Operating margin 22.3%, and Profit margin 14.2% are strong.
Return on Equity 34.0% and Return on Assets 17.2% demonstrate efficient use of capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $824.08M provides ample liquidity.
negatives: Revenue growth is a mere 0.8% YoY.
Earnings growth is negative at -18.5% YoY.
Quarterly earnings growth is significantly down at -21.6%.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter (-35.0%) and fiscal year (-7.2%) are negative.
Debt/Equity ratio of 36.24% is moderate but warrants attention given the earnings decline.

news.paragraph: The provided headline discusses AI compute costs for a film at Cannes, which is unrelated to Lululemon's business operations. There is no recent news directly impacting Lululemon's stock in the provided context. The upcoming earnings report on June 4th will be the next significant event to watch for directional catalysts.

headlines.title: This Cannes Film Cost $500,000 to Make. $400,000 Was AI Compute Costs.

examples.body: $LULU $NKE Congratulations to all. Medical costs are crushing us financially. If anyone can donate or even share, it would mean everything to our family ❤️ htt…
